mnemonics: PMF is for 'Possible, Measurable Fractions' (discrete), while PDF is for 'Possible, Dense Fabric' (continuous) - you need to integrate to get an area/scrap of the fabric.
Expectation is just a 'Weighted Average' - multiply each outcome by its probability (or density) and sum (or integrate) it all up.
keyPoints: A Probability Mass Function (PMF) gives the probability that a discrete random variable takes on a specific value.
A Probability Density Function (PDF) describes the relative likelihood for a continuous random variable to take on a given value; probability is found by integrating the PDF over an interval.
Mathematical Expectation (the mean) is the long-run average value of the random variable, calculated as Σ [x * P(x)] for discrete variables and ∫ [x * f(x) dx] for continuous variables.
commonMistakes: Treating the value of a PDF, f(x), as a probability. f(x) itself is not a probability; the probability is the area under the curve.
Using a summation (Σ) for a continuous variable or an integral (∫) for a discrete variable when calculating expectation.
Confusing the properties: PMFs must sum to 1, while PDFs must integrate to 1 over all possible values.
